---
title: "Status Update at Pismo Monarch Grove"
date: "2023-10-29"
author: "Kyle Nessen"
format: html
editor: visual
---

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Summary

I visit the grove yesterday to download data, map sensor locations, determine how many are stuck, and test methods to get stuck sensors down.

```{r}
library(RSQLite)
library(dplyr)

get_db_file_paths <- function(directory_path, file_extension = "s3db") {
  db_files <- list.files(path = directory_path, pattern = paste0("\\.", file_extension, "$"), full.names = TRUE)
  return(db_files)
}

# Function to list tables in a database file
list_tables_in_db <- function(db_path) {
  con <- dbConnect(RSQLite::SQLite(), dbname = db_path)
  tables <- dbListTables(con)
  dbDisconnect(con)
  return(tables)
}

# Usage
directory_path <- "~/Documents/Code/masters/memos/data/sensors"
db_file_paths <- get_db_file_paths(directory_path)

# List tables in the first database file to verify the table names
if(length(db_file_paths) > 0) {
  table_names <- list_tables_in_db(db_file_paths[1])
  print(table_names)
}

# Make sure to replace 'your_table_name' with the actual table name from the printout above
table_name <- "actual_table_name_here"

# Function to read and label data
read_and_label_data <- function(db_path, table_name) {
  con <- dbConnect(RSQLite::SQLite(), dbname = db_path)
  df <- dbReadTable(con, table_name)
  dbDisconnect(con)
  sensorID <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(db_path))
  df$sensorID <- sensorID
  return(df)
}

# Initialize an empty list to store dataframes
df_list <- list()

# Loop over db_files and read each database file into a dataframe
for(db_path in db_file_paths) {
  df <- read_and_label_data(db_path, table_name)
  df_list[[db_path]] <- df
}

# Combine all dataframes into a single dataframe
combined_df <- bind_rows(df_list)

# Now 'combined_df' contains all the data with 'sensorID'

```

```{python}
print('hello world')
```
